text:
It's a beautiful autumn afternoon. I'm sitting by a window, watching the leaf colors change. In the distance, a squirrel scurries along a branch. It pauses at a ripe apple and starts chipping away. Maybe it'll take that first bite.

Suddenly, it stops. Something's got its attention. It looks up and spots a hawk perched above. The squirrel scurries away quietly. The hawk tilts its head. I imagine it's pondering how tiny that little squirrel must look.

I go out. There's a fallen leaf crunching beneath my feet. I hop over the fence like the squirrel. A crow watches. Maybe it's the same hunter. I shake my feathers free.

Back at the apple tree, I find a perfectly ripe kernel. The squirrel hops down happily.

Back in my den, I nibble on fruit. I whistle a cheerful tune.

That evening, I dream. I'm back by the window, watching a starry night.
